claims.quote: Today, we train more than 1,200 house staff in over 180 residency and fellowship programs.
claims.claimText: VUMC trains more than 1,200 house staff in over 180 residency and fellowship programs.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: GME office statement of scope of residency/fellowship training — Tier 2 trainee programs.

claims.quote: The Office of Graduate Medical Education is committed to supporting the trainees, faculty, and staff in our many residency and fellowship programs for new physicians.
claims.claimText: GME office supports residency and fellowship programs for new physicians at VUMC.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: GME office mission — residency/fellowship Tier 2.
claims.visibilityRationale: source family GME_PAGE is future-lane only

claims.quote: If you would like to apply for a residency or fellowship at VUMC, visit our applicants page .

claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Residency/fellowship application pathway — Tier 2.
claims.visibilityRationale: source family GME_PAGE is future-lane only

claims.quote: Residency training began here with just 12 residents in 1925.
claims.claimText: VUMC residency training started in 1925 with 12 residents.

claims.quote: Nurse Residency Program
claims.claimText: Careers portal references a Nurse Residency Program (nursing-only, not physician GME).

claims.quote: We value the experiences that visiting residents/fellows bring to our program, and we want to make sure that house staff from other institutions have every opportunity to come to VUMC and experience what we have to offer. Visiting rotations are subject to approval by the appropriate Program Director.
claims.claimText: VUMC offers visiting rotations for residents/fellows from other institutions, subject to Program Director approval.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Page is explicitly for visiting residents/fellows (house staff), which is Tier 2 trainee rotations, not pre-residency USCE.
claims.visibilityRationale: lane is future-lane only

claims.quote: To be eligible for a visiting rotation, you must be in an ACGME-accredited training program and must qualify for licensure or exemption from licensure under the Tennessee Board of Medical Examiners requirements
claims.claimText: Eligibility requires being in an ACGME-accredited training program and qualifying for Tennessee licensure or exemption.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Eligibility statement is for residents/fellows in ACGME-accredited programs — Tier 2.
claims.visibilityRationale: lane is future-lane only

claims.quote: Visiting resident applicants must apply 120 days or more from start date and have met all requirements 30 days or more prior to their requested start date. if this requirement is not met, the visiting resident's request to rotate will be cancelled and they will need to reapply.
claims.claimText: Visiting residents must apply ≥120 days before start date and meet all requirements ≥30 days prior.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Application timeline applies to visiting residents — Tier 2.
claims.visibilityRationale: lane is future-lane only

claims.quote: The Program Coordinator of the VUMC program with which you desire to rotate must initiate a contract /program letter of agreement process at least 100 days before your desired start date .
claims.claimText: Program Coordinator must initiate program letter of agreement ≥100 days before start date.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Process is part of visiting resident onboarding — Tier 2.
claims.visibilityRationale: lane is future-lane only

claims.quote: A completed and signed visiting resident application Click here for application . This will need to be approved by the VUMC Program Director. The application requires your NPI number.
claims.claimText: A signed visiting resident application requiring NPI number, approved by VUMC Program Director, is required.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Application document requirements for visiting residents — Tier 2.
claims.visibilityRationale: lane is future-lane only

claims.quote: A reference letter from your Program Director stating whether you are in good standing in an accredited training program.
claims.claimText: A reference letter from home Program Director attesting to good standing is required.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Document requirement for visiting resident rotation — Tier 2.
claims.visibilityRationale: lane is future-lane only

claims.quote: Documentation that your institution will continue to provide your stipend along with benefits, liability and health insurance (can be in the same letter as above).
claims.claimText: Home institution must document continued stipend, benefits, liability, and health insurance during rotation.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Document requirement for visiting resident — Tier 2.
claims.visibilityRationale: lane is future-lane only

claims.quote: A copy of your malpractice liability insurance with a minimum of $1 million per claimant and $3 million per occurrence, with the exception that Tennessee state employees do not need additional coverage.
claims.claimText: Malpractice insurance of ≥$1M/claimant and $3M/occurrence required (TN state employees exempt).
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Malpractice insurance requirement for visiting residents — Tier 2.
claims.visibilityRationale: lane is future-lane only

claims.quote: If you are in a residency/fellowship in Tennessee and do not have your own medical license, a copy of the letter of exemption from licensure (your program or GME Office will have this). Note: If you are not in a Tennessee residency/fellowship and do not have full TN medical license, VUMC will request an exemption from licensure for you.
claims.claimText: Licensure or licensure-exemption letter required; VUMC will request exemption for non-TN residents without TN license.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Licensure requirement for visiting residents — Tier 2.
claims.visibilityRationale: lane is future-lane only

claims.quote: If applicable, a copy of a valid ECFMG certificate and Visa.
claims.claimText: Valid ECFMG certificate and visa required if applicable.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: ECFMG/visa requirement for IMG visiting residents — Tier 2.
claims.visibilityRationale: lane is future-lane only

claims.quote: Immunization Form. This form must be completed by a healthcare provider other than the perspective visiting resident/fellow and sent to the Program Coordinator of the program with which you will rotate.
claims.claimText: Immunization Form completed by a healthcare provider (not self) is required.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Immunization document requirement for visiting residents/fellows — Tier 2.
claims.visibilityRationale: lane is future-lane only

claims.quote: Completion of the consent form from Cisive will initiate the background check. Cisive will contact you with a web link to initiate your background check.
claims.claimText: Cisive-administered background check is required.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Background check requirement for visiting residents — Tier 2.
claims.visibilityRationale: lane is future-lane only

claims.quote: please feel free to contact Lisa (615-322-4916 or lisa.king-rowan@vumc.org ) or our office email ( gme.office@vumc.org ).

claims.quote: You must check in with the GME office in person on day one of your rotation.

claims.quote: Your application must be submitted to the VUMC program at least 120 days beforehand and supporting documents (items 1-6, below) must be submitted to the VUMC program at least 90 days beforehand so that the rotation is fully onboarded 30 days from start date.
claims.claimText: Supporting documents (items 1-6) must be submitted ≥90 days before start date; rotation fully onboarded 30 days prior.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Supporting-document 90-day deadline is distinct from A1's 120-day application deadline — Tier 2.
claims.whyA1Missed: A1 captured the 120-day application and 100-day PLA deadlines but missed the separate 90-day supporting-documents deadline.

claims.quote: If applicable, a copy of a valid ECFMG certificate and Visa.You must complete the following at least 60 days in advance:

claims.quote: Office of Inspector General/Excluded Individuals - In order to comply with federal law, VUMC will check all visiting house staff against the Office of Inspector General's list of individuals excluded from federal healthcare programs. Any visiting house staff identified as excluded must be terminated from the visiting residency training program and home institution will be notified.
claims.claimText: VUMC checks all visiting house staff against OIG excluded-individuals list; excluded individuals are terminated.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: OIG exclusion screening for visiting residents — Tier 2 trainee screening requirement.
claims.whyA1Missed: A1 captured the Cisive background check but missed the separate OIG database screening requirement.
claims.visibilityRationale: lane is future-lane only

claims.quote: National Practitioners Data Bank (NPDB) - All visiting house staff will be checked against the NPDB.
